description: You are a storage administrator who has been asked to reclaim space in the application log area /home/user/storage/logs. Only files that end with the extension “.tmp” must be removed; all other files have to stay intact. Requirements (all must be satisfied): 1. Use exactly one terminal command that combines find and xargs (you may add other standard utilities such as tee, rm, an inline sh -c, etc.), but the whole action has to be triggered by a single line that is pasted into the shell. 2. The command has to: a. Search recursively under the directory /home/user/storage/logs. b. Delete every “.tmp” file it finds. c. While deleting, create the log file /home/user/deleted_tmp.log. 3. The log file must obey this exact format (the test-suite checks it): • Plain UTF-8 text. • One and only one absolute path per line. • Each line must be the full path to a “.tmp” file that was deleted. • No blank lines, no leading or trailing spaces or tabs. • The file has to end with a single new-line character (UNIX “\n”). 4. After your command finishes: • Every “.tmp” file that originally existed under the target directory must be gone. • All non-“.tmp” files must still be present and unmodified. • The log file must be present and meet the format described above. Verification notes (what the grader will inspect): • It will confirm that /home/user/deleted_tmp.log exists and is readable. • It will read the file and compare the set of logged paths to the set of “.tmp” files that existed before your command ran (order does not matter, but the paths themselves must match exactly and there must be no extras). • It will assert that none of the logged paths still exists on disk. • It will confirm that files not ending in “.tmp” are still present. Hint (not the exact command): find … -type f -name '*.tmp' -print | tee … | xargs … Deliverable: Just run the single command in the terminal; no additional explanation is needed. Once that is done, the task is considered complete.
truth:
<!--
        Initial on-disk layout before the agent starts.
        All paths are absolute and already exist with read/write permissions
        for user “user”.
    -->
    <initialState>
        <directory path="/home/user/storage/logs">
            <file name="root.tmp"/>
            <file name="keep.log"/>
            <directory name="app1">
                <file name="upload.tmp"/>
                <file name="session.tmp"/>
                <file name="info.log"/>
            </directory>
            <directory name="app2">
                <file name="error.log"/>
                <directory name="archive">
                    <file name="cache.tmp"/>
                    <file name="old_2021.tmp"/>
                </directory>
            </directory>
        </directory>
    </initialState>

    <!--
        Verification data.
        The grader expects these five exact paths to be deleted and listed
        in the log file, nothing more and nothing less.
    -->
    <tmpFiles>
        <path>/home/user/storage/logs/root.tmp</path>
        <path>/home/user/storage/logs/app1/upload.tmp</path>
        <path>/home/user/storage/logs/app1/session.tmp</path>
        <path>/home/user/storage/logs/app2/archive/cache.tmp</path>
        <path>/home/user/storage/logs/app2/archive/old_2021.tmp</path>
    </tmpFiles>

    <logFile>/home/user/deleted_tmp.log</logFile>

    <!--
        Final state expected after the agent’s command completes:
        • Each path mentioned in <tmpFiles> no longer exists.
        • Every other file from <initialState> is still present.
        • <logFile> exists and contains exactly five lines, each line being
          one of the <tmpFiles> paths (order arbitrary), with no extra
          whitespace and ending with a single "\n".
    -->
name: task_000000_6c703b06
